# DataCoolie

> Metadata-driven ETL framework for Python — engine-unified, cloud-agnostic, batch-first.

DataCoolie lets data engineering teams describe pipeline intent once as metadata
(JSON / YAML / Excel / database / REST API) and execute on Polars, Spark, Fabric,
Databricks, or AWS Glue without rewriting per-engine code. Version: 0.1.2.

## Key Features

- **Engine-unified**: Same metadata runs on both Polars and Spark via `BaseEngine[DF]` contract
- **Cloud-agnostic**: Local, AWS, Microsoft Fabric, and Databricks platforms with zero code changes
- **Metadata-driven**: Connections, dataflows, transforms, schema hints, partitions, and load strategies are declarative
- **Right-sized compute**: Use Polars for small/medium jobs, Spark for distributed workloads
- **Batch-first**: append, full_load, merge_upsert, merge_overwrite, and SCD Type 2 built-in
- **Lakehouse-native**: First-class Delta Lake and Apache Iceberg support
- **Plugin everything**: Engines, platforms, sources, destinations, transformers, secret resolvers are all entry-point plugins
- **Observable by default**: Structured ETLLogger with debug JSONL and analyst Parquet outputs

## When to Use DataCoolie

- Building ETL pipelines that must run on multiple engines (Polars locally, Spark in production)
- Teams that need cloud portability (Fabric ↔ Databricks ↔ AWS ↔ local)
- Projects where pipeline configuration should be declarative, not imperative code
- Replacing per-engine boilerplate with a single metadata-driven framework
- Data teams that want AI agents to scaffold, validate, and evolve pipelines

## Comparison with Other Tools

- **vs dbt**: DataCoolie is a Python ETL execution layer; dbt is SQL-first transforms. They complement each other.
- **vs Airflow/Prefect**: DataCoolie handles read→transform→write→watermark inside jobs. Airflow/Prefect orchestrate when jobs run. Use DataCoolie inside Airflow DAGs.
- **vs custom PySpark/Polars scripts**: DataCoolie replaces the boilerplate patterns you rewrite per-job with declarative metadata.

## Install

```bash
pip install "datacoolie[polars]"      # Polars engine
pip install "datacoolie[spark,delta]" # Spark engine with Delta Lake
```

## Documentation

- [Home](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/): Overview, value proposition, and quickstart
- [Getting Started](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/getting-started/): Installation, Polars quickstart, Spark quickstart, first dataflow tutorial
- [Concepts](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/concepts/): Architecture, engines, platforms, metadata model, orchestration, logging, secrets, watermarks
- [How-to Guides](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/how-to/): Configuring metadata, running stages, merge/SCD2, partitioning, maintenance, deployment recipes
- [Reference](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/reference/): Metadata schema, plugin entry points, CLI, environment variables, full Python API
- [Extending](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/extending/): Plugin development — sources, destinations, transformers, engines, secret resolvers, metadata providers
- [Operations](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/operations/): Production guidance, logging layout, benchmarks, troubleshooting, testing strategy
- [ADR](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/adr/): Architecture decision records for engine format dispatch, secret provider split, transformer ordering, watermark contract
- [Changelog](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/changelog/): Version history and release notes
- [Blog](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/blog/): Architecture posts, tutorials, and release notes

## Source Code

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/datacoolie/datacoolie): Source code, issues, and contributions
- [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/datacoolie/): Package index page
